title: meniscium lanceum
intro: Meniscium lanceum, also known as lance-leaved meniscus or meniscium, is a type of flowering plant in the family Pteridaceae, native to New Zealand. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and promoting wound healing. Meniscium lanceum can be prepared as a herbal remedy through infusion, decoction, or tincture, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
